title: Database query result table with column resize + reorder
design_brief: Building SQL query result table (think TablePlus, Beekeeper Studio, DBeaver). Pain point: column widths and order are user preferences that don't persist.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Reset to default on every query
ux_reasoning: Each column header has a 4px-wide resize handle on right edge. Drag to resize. Drag column header to reorder. Persist widths + order per query via localStorage.
interaction_details: Hover column right edge: cursor changes to col-resize.
Column reorder via drag header (similar drag impl).
accessibility_considerations: Resize via keyboard (Cmd+Arrow on focused header).
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Per-query persistence — every query remembers its layout. 2) Resize handle is 4px (precise grab). 3) min-width prevents collapse.

title: Quick switcher (Cmd+P) for files/pages with recent first
design_brief: Building quick file switcher (think VS Code Cmd+P, Cursor). Pain point: most file pickers don't surface recent files.
ux_reasoning: Cmd+P opens. Shows recent 8 files first; type to fuzzy-match across all. Enter opens; Tab to next file with same name.
accessibility_considerations: role='dialog'/'listbox'/'option'.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Recent-first when no query — most-used UX. 2) Search across name AND path. 3) Section header for recent.

title: Multi-cursor edit in plain text input
design_brief: Building a shared text input (think collab branch names, multi-user form). Pain point: cursors don't show in plain inputs.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic 'last writer wins' with no preview
ux_reasoning: Show colored caret markers from other users at their cursor positions in the input. Each marker labelled with name on hover. Text remains editable for all.
interaction_details: Other users' cursors shown as colored vertical lines in the input.
accessibility_considerations: Carets aria-hidden. Names available in collaborator list elsewhere.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Per-character caret position (using mono char width). 2) Color from per-user palette. 3) Doesn't lock the input.

title: Checkbox with smooth check-draw animation
design_brief: Building a checkbox primitive (think Apple, Linear). Pain point: most checkboxes either snap or have generic bounce.
ux_reasoning: Custom checkbox. On check: checkmark path draws in over 200ms via stroke-dashoffset. Bg fills simultaneously. Uncheck: instant.

design_tokens.colors: Checked bg: brand. Unchecked: oklch(0.93 0.005 250) border.

interaction_details: Check: bg fills + checkmark draws in over 200ms.
accessibility_considerations: Real checkbox input. Label is real label.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Stroke-dashoffset draw — the checkmark APPEARS, not snaps. 2) Real <input> hidden via sr-only. 3) 200ms is precise.

title: Box plot with quartile rendering and outlier dots
design_brief: Building box plot (think performance distributions, test scores). Pain point: rare in product UIs but powerful.
ux_reasoning: Each group: box from Q1 to Q3, median line, whiskers to 1.5*IQR, outliers as dots. Hover any element: detail tooltip.

design_tokens.colors: Box: oklch(0.55 0.18 250) at 30% fill. Median: brand 2px. Outlier dots: brand.

interaction_details: Box from Q1-Q3, median line, whiskers to 1.5*IQR.
accessibility_considerations: SR table with full distribution stats.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real statistical box plot (not bar). 2) Outliers visible. 3) Whisker rule is correct (1.5*IQR).

title: Demo workspace tour via auto-played guided cursor
design_brief: Building optional product demo for landing page (think Linear's homepage tour). Pain point: video demos are passive; interactive demos require user clicks.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Required user clicks at each step
ux_reasoning: Visitor sees a real product preview. A simulated cursor moves through the UI demonstrating key actions automatically. Click anywhere to take control.

design_tokens.motion: Cursor moves: cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1) over 1-2s per move.

interaction_details: Click anywhere: cursor disappears, user takes over.
accessibility_considerations: aria-hidden. Sequence has equivalent SR-readable narration alongside.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Click-to-takeover — visitor never trapped. 2) Smooth cursor animation simulates a real user. 3) Loops if visitor watches.

title: File-type input with drop and paste support
design_brief: Building file input that also accepts paste (think Linear's attachment paste, GitHub's drag-image-to-issue). Pain point: most file inputs ignore paste.
ux_reasoning: Input accepts: click (native picker), drop (dropzone visible), paste (Ctrl+V even when focused elsewhere on the dropzone).
interaction_details: Paste anywhere on page: captures clipboard files (Linear pattern).
accessibility_considerations: Real file input. Drop is supplementary.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Paste anywhere on page — pasted screenshots auto-attach. 2) Three input methods in one component. 3) Real <input type='file'> for accessibility.

title: Story scene that pans through 3D as user scrolls
design_brief: Building a scrollytelling 3D experience (think Apple AirPods page, NASA mission pages). Pain point: most scroll-3D is jumpy.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Camera moves that ignore scroll velocity
ux_reasoning: Camera position locked to scroll progress along a Bezier path. Per-section captions sticky-left. Smooth even on touch scroll.

design_tokens.motion: Camera follows scroll directly (no lerp — scroll IS the velocity).

interaction_details: Camera position tracks scroll progress through Bezier curve.
Frame-perfect on touch scroll (scroll IS the timeline).
accessibility_considerations: Captions are real text. Reduced-motion: camera snaps to section anchors.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Catmull-Rom for smooth path. 2) Direct scroll binding (no lerp lag). 3) Sticky captions preserve readability.

title: Sticky action bar on mobile, inline on desktop
design_brief: Building primary action UI (think 'Add to cart', 'Send' button). Pain point: same UI doesn't work for both.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Inline button below fold on mobile
ux_reasoning: Mobile: sticky bottom bar (above safe-area-inset) with primary action full-width. Desktop: inline button in form/page flow.

design_tokens.colors: Sticky bar bg: white with shadow.
design_tokens.spacing: Mobile bar: 64px + safe-area. Desktop button: inline.

interaction_details: Mobile: bottom sticky bar with safe-area padding.
accessibility_considerations: Real button. Sticky bar doesn't trap focus.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Two paradigms from one component. 2) iPhone safe-area-inset handled. 3) Single source of truth for primary action.

title: Radio group following arrow-key roving tabindex
design_brief: Building radio group (think any settings page). Pain point: many use native radio (no styling control) or div with toggleable bg (broken keyboard).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Each radio a tab stop (slow nav)
ux_reasoning: Roving tabindex: single tab stop into group. Arrow keys move selection. Wrap at ends.
interaction_details: Tab into group: focuses selected radio.
accessibility_considerations: Full WAI-ARIA radio pattern: role='radiogroup'/'radio', aria-checked, roving tabindex.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Roving tabindex — single tab stop for the group. 2) Arrow keys both navigate AND select (radio convention). 3) Real ARIA roles.

title: Lazy-mount tabs with pre-render adjacent
design_brief: Building tabs where content is heavy (think Settings panels, analytics charts). Pain point: all tabs render on mount = slow.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Lost scroll position when switching back
ux_reasoning: Mount only the active tab + adjacent (1 left, 1 right). When user switches, adjacent is already mounted. Inactive tabs persist scroll state.
interaction_details: Non-adjacent: lazy mount on first switch.
accessibility_considerations: role='tablist'/'tab'/'tabpanel'. hidden for non-active.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Adjacent pre-mount makes switching instant. 2) Persist scroll across switches. 3) Never all-tabs-at-once.

title: Glassmorphism done with restraint and depth
design_brief: Building a glass-style overlay (think Apple Vision Pro, macOS Big Sur). Pain point: 'glassmorphism' is often misapplied as random blurs everywhere.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Glass on light bg with no contrast
ux_reasoning: Glass works when there's REAL content behind it that's blurred. Use backdrop-filter: blur(40px) saturate(180%) for the Vision OS look. Subtle 1px border at 20% white inside.

design_tokens.colors: Glass bg: oklch(0.98 0.005 250 / 0.6). Border: white at 20%.

interaction_details: Used over real blurred content (image, gradient bg).
accessibility_considerations: Ensure text on glass has fallback bg color for accessibility (forced-colors mode).
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) backdrop-filter blur + saturate — period-correct Vision OS look. 2) Subtle 1px inner-border highlight. 3) Soft shadow grounds the panel.

title: Server status grid (region heat) with timeline-collapse
design_brief: Building status page (think GitHub Status, Vercel Status). Pain point: long history shown as multiple charts; hard to see overall.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Long flat history of green bars
ux_reasoning: Grid: services in rows, last 90 days as columns (collapsed by week). Each cell shows max severity in that period. Click cell: detail panel.

design_tokens.colors: Operational: oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Degraded: oklch(0.75 0.16 80). Outage: oklch(0.6 0.18 25).

interaction_details: Hover any square for date + status.
accessibility_considerations: Each cell has title + aria-label.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) 90-day heatmap per service — long history at a glance. 2) Colors meaningful (3 levels). 3) Hover for detail.

title: Date picker with arrow keys + Cmd+T for today
design_brief: Building accessible date picker (think Stripe, Google). Pain point: most date pickers fail keyboard nav.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Arrow keys move month not day
ux_reasoning: Arrow keys move day. PageUp/Down month; Cmd+PageUp/Down year. Cmd+T jumps to today. Enter commits.

design_tokens.colors: Today: ring brand. Selected: bg brand.

interaction_details: Arrow keys move 1 day; up/down by week.
accessibility_considerations: role='grid'. Single tabindex on container; cells are buttons.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cmd+T for today — power-user shortcut. 2) PageUp/Down for month — keyboard standard. 3) Today is ring, selected is fill — distinct.

title: Avatar stack with overflow tooltip
design_brief: Building avatar stack (think Notion's presence, Linear's project members). Pain point: 'X others' tooltip is generic.
ux_reasoning: Show 4 avatars max; overflow shows '+N' in last position. Hover stack: tooltip lists all members.
interaction_details: Title attribute per avatar for sighted hover.
accessibility_considerations: Real img with alt. Tooltip is sighted-only; full list elsewhere for SR.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Tooltip on hover lists everyone — never lost. 2) -8px overlap mimics chat-app convention. 3) Title attribute per avatar for individual context.

title: Tooltip with arrow that points at trigger
design_brief: Building a tooltip primitive (think Radix UI Tooltip). Pain point: most tooltips lack the arrow.
ux_reasoning: On hover/focus after 350ms intent delay, tooltip appears above (or repositioned to fit). Triangular arrow connects to trigger.
interaction_details: Triangle arrow points down at trigger.
accessibility_considerations: role='tooltip'. Triggered by focus too (not just hover).
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Intent delay matches industry standard. 2) CSS triangle arrow (no SVG). 3) Focus also triggers (keyboard users).

title: Multi-series candle chart with hover crosshair
design_brief: Building OHLC candle chart for trading (think TradingView). Pain point: candles need precise hover info.
ux_reasoning: Per-candle: green/red body for open/close direction, wick for high/low. Hover crosshair shows OHLC values at top.
accessibility_considerations: SR data table with candles.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Standard OHLC candle rendering. 2) Crosshair on hover for precise read. 3) Two-color directional encoding.

title: Personalization quiz collapsed into the app sidebar
design_brief: Building first-run preferences (think 'what do you want to see first?'). Pain point: modal quiz before app use.
ux_reasoning: Quiz appears as a collapsible card in the sidebar. User can answer one question at a time, skip, or hide. App is fully usable throughout.
interaction_details: Card in sidebar with current question.
accessibility_considerations: role='complementary'. Real buttons.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Sidebar card, not modal — app usable throughout. 2) One question, not 10. 3) Skip and Hide both supported.

title: Range slider with two thumbs for min/max
design_brief: Building dual-thumb range (think Airbnb price range, Spotify mood). Pain point: most implementations don't handle keyboard nav well.
ux_reasoning: Two thumbs, can't cross. Tab to either; arrow keys adjust. Range fill between thumbs.

design_tokens.colors: Track: oklch(0.92 0.005 250). Range fill: brand. Thumb: white with brand ring.

interaction_details: Tab to either; arrow keys adjust.
accessibility_considerations: Each thumb is role='slider' with aria-valuenow.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Two real ARIA sliders — independently keyboard-navigable. 2) Cross-prevention. 3) Range fill visualizes selection.

title: AR product preview button on mobile
design_brief: Building 'View in AR' for e-commerce (think Apple ARKit Quick Look). Pain point: AR features are often hidden.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: AR button only on detail page
Loading spinner instead of preview while AR launches
ux_reasoning: Use <model-viewer> with ar attribute. Button is prominent on mobile (where AR works), hidden on desktop. Falls back gracefully if device doesn't support.
interaction_details: AR button shown only if device supports.
Click: launches native AR (iOS uses .usdz Quick Look).
accessibility_considerations: Real anchor with descriptive text.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) AR support detection. 2) iOS USDZ Quick Look (no app needed). 3) Hidden on unsupported devices (not broken).

title: Form layout: single column mobile, two-column desktop
design_brief: Building forms (think Stripe Checkout, Shopify). Pain point: same single column on desktop wastes space.
ux_reasoning: Mobile: single column, every field full width. Desktop ≥768px: two-col grid for related fields (city + state, first + last name).
interaction_details: Desktop: 2-col grid; fields can span 2 cols for emails/notes.
accessibility_considerations: Real form semantics. Tab order matches DOM.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Smart use of desktop space without breaking mobile. 2) span prop for full-width fields. 3) CSS grid handles layout — no JS.

title: Listbox with multi-select via Shift and Ctrl per ARIA
design_brief: Building accessible multi-select listbox (think OS file picker, Apple Music). Pain point: most listbox implementations skip ARIA multi-select.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic 'click to toggle' without OS conventions
ux_reasoning: role='listbox' aria-multiselectable='true'. Click selects single. Shift+click extends range. Cmd/Ctrl+click toggles individual. aria-selected per option.
interaction_details: Click single, Shift+click range, Cmd+click toggle individual.
accessibility_considerations: Full ARIA multi-select pattern.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) aria-multiselectable correctly set. 2) Honors OS conventions for multi-select. 3) Anchor preserved for range extends.

title: Progressive enhancement: server-rendered first paint
design_brief: Building SSR-first React app (think Next.js, Remix). Pain point: client-only apps blank-flash on load.
ux_reasoning: Server renders content first. Client hydrates seamlessly. Critical CSS inlined. Suspense streams non-critical chunks.
interaction_details: Server returns full HTML with content.
accessibility_considerations: Content is in HTML first — SR and search engines see it.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) SSR-first means first paint has content. 2) Suspense streams without blocking. 3) Skeleton matches real shape.

title: Tactile haptic-inspired button with subtle press animation
design_brief: Building primary action button (think Apple Pay button, Things 3 add). Pain point: most buttons are flat or shadow-only.
ux_reasoning: Button has slight inner shadow + soft outer shadow. On press: shadow inverts (button 'depresses'). Subtle 50ms only.

design_tokens.colors: Bg: brand. Shadow: inset top + outer bottom on rest. Reversed on press.

interaction_details: Rest: subtle inner highlight + outer shadow (raised).
accessibility_considerations: Real button. Keyboard activation also triggers press state.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Inset + outset shadow combo simulates physical button. 2) 50ms is fast enough to feel responsive. 3) Subtle, not exaggerated.

title: Drag-and-drop kanban with column WIP limits
design_brief: Building kanban with limits (think Trello with Power-Ups, Linear cycles). Pain point: WIP limits invisible.
ux_reasoning: Each column shows current count / WIP limit. When over limit, column header turns warning color but drops still allowed (soft limit).
interaction_details: Over limit: header text turns warning color.
Drops still allowed (soft warning, not hard block).
accessibility_considerations: Real heading. Limits announced via aria.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Visible WIP limit per column. 2) Soft warning, not block — agile-friendly. 3) Color encodes state.

title: App switcher with Cmd+1-9 to jump to recent workspaces
design_brief: Building workspace switcher (think Slack workspace switcher, VS Code window switcher). Pain point: switching is many clicks.
ux_reasoning: Cmd+1 to Cmd+9 switches to corresponding workspace in the rail. Cmd+0 opens picker for further ones. Recent on top.

design_tokens.colors: Workspace icon: per-workspace color. Active: ring.

accessibility_considerations: Real nav + buttons. Title attribute exposes shortcut.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cmd+1-9 — universal keyboard pattern. 2) Workspace color is identity. 3) Tooltip shows shortcut for discoverability.

title: Activity counter on tab when window blurred
design_brief: Building unread counter in browser tab (think Slack, Gmail). Pain point: many sites ignore document.title hint.
ux_reasoning: While window is blurred and unread messages arrive: update document.title prefix '(3) Original'. Clear when window regains focus or user reads all.

design_tokens.colors: Tab favicon: brand. Counter is text in title.

interaction_details: When tab hidden + unread > 0: prepend count to title.
accessibility_considerations: Native title attribute, supported by all browsers/SR.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Only updates while hidden — no flashing for active users. 2) Native title prefix — no library. 3) Clears on refocus automatically.

title: Bookmark/save toggle with morphing icon
design_brief: Building bookmark button (think Twitter bookmark, Medium save). Pain point: outline → filled toggle is sudden.
ux_reasoning: Icon is single SVG path with fill animating from transparent to brand. 200ms transition. Hover: subtle scale 1.05.
accessibility_considerations: aria-pressed + aria-label.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Fill transition, not swap. 2) Single SVG with fill attribute animation. 3) Subtle hover scale for affordance.

title: Lollipop chart for ranked comparison (bar chart alternative)
design_brief: Building ranked viz (think simplified bar chart for narrow data). Pain point: bars feel heavy for sparse data.
ux_reasoning: Each category: thin line + circle at end. Lighter visual weight than bars. Sorted ranked.
interaction_details: Each item: thin line + brand dot.
accessibility_considerations: Real list semantics.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Lollipop is lighter than bar — more elegant for sparse data. 2) Real <ul> semantics. 3) Sorted by default.

title: App entrance: skip 'splash' screen, go directly to last visited
design_brief: Building app launch experience (think Linear, Notion). Pain point: many apps show splash + 'welcome' before content.
ux_reasoning: On launch, restore last-visited route from localStorage. No splash. First-time users go to a 'getting started' route (still no modal).
interaction_details: Every route change saves to localStorage.
Next launch: navigate to that route.
accessibility_considerations: Standard router. No animations.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Persistent last route — relaunch resumes context. 2) No splash. 3) New users get content-driven start.
